items.title: Critical RCE and Unauthenticated File Upload in Amasty Extensions
items.summary: Amasty released a massive security update for 25 extensions, including a critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability and an unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw (CVE-2026-53787) in Amasty Order Attributes. These vulnerabilities allow attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, allowing remote code execution via a crafted cookie. The flaw, rated 9.8 CVSS, was patched by Mirasvit on May 25, 2026, and added to CISA's KEV catalog due to active exploitation.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce (Incorrect Authorization)
items.summary: Adobe released APSB26-49 addressing CVE-2026-34646, a critical Incorrect Authorization vulnerability in Adobe Commerce. This flaw allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to bypass security checks and gain unauthorized write access to the affected application.

items.title: Unrestricted File Upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered PolyShell, a critical vulnerability in Magento's REST API that enables unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files by disguising malicious code as an image. A fix was released in Magento 2.4.9 on May 12, 2026, but older supported versions remain vulnerable.

items.title: OpenMage Magento RCE Vulnerability via Incomplete Blocklist in File Upload
items.summary: CVE-2026-40488 is a critical remote code execution flaw in OpenMage Magento LTS, stemming from inadequate file upload validation that uses an incomplete blocklist. Authenticated attackers can upload malicious PHP files to achieve RCE.

items.title: Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released APSB26-05, a critical security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, addressing multiple vulnerabilities. Successful exploitation could lead to security feature bypass, privilege escalation, and arbitrary code execution.
